content.question: How do Hungary's specific contextual features—centralized governance, free school choice without catchment enforcement, and Roma minority residential concentration—alter the applicability of early-tracking-reduction models from decentralized or more ethnically homogeneous countries?
content.reduction_path: Conduct comparative institutional analysis mapping governance structure, choice regime, and ethnic demographics across Hungary, Germany, Poland, and Scandinavian cases; use qualitative case studies to identify which institutional mechanisms enabled or blocked reform success in each context.
